name: Tuvan
notes: Tuvan (тыва дыл) — Siberian Turkic language of the Tyva Republic, Russia. Written in a Cyrillic-based Tuvan alphabet (the Russian alphabet plus ң, ө, ү); a Latin alphabet was used 1930–1943. GRAPHEME INVENTORY NOT YET SPECIFIED: a reliable spec must model the Cyrillic palatalisation/iotation conventions, vowel harmony, phonemic vowel length (written by doubling) and the pharyngealised ('kargyraa') vowel series, which is not consistently marked in the orthography. INPUT CONTRACT: input is expected in the Cyrillic Tuvan orthography. PHONEME INVENTORY: attested inventory from PHOIBLE (glottocode tuvi1240), declared directly. `graphemes` stays empty because none of that depends on the writing system: the sounds of this language are known regardless of whether — or how — it is written.

sources.author: Moran, Steven & McCloy, Daniel (eds.)
sources.title: PHOIBLE 2.0
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for the Science of Human History
sources.notes: Attested phoneme inventory for glottocode tuvi1240.
